page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. It features an arm64 architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 model. The CPU includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It also features a bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed temporary storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals average performance in compute-heavy workloads, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores reaching 1519.58 and 6061.97 respectively. Memory bandwidth is mixed, showing average performance on large block reads at 72.54 GB/sec and writes at 33.66 GB/sec, but weaker performance on small block cached reads at 235.91 GB/sec. Passmark database operations perform at an average level of 2607.7, while other metrics like Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU score rank in the weak to poor categories. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also show lower relative performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6gd.xlarge offers a balanced entry point for ARM-based compute workloads requiring local NVMe storage without the overhead of hyperthreading, as it features a 1:1 physical-to-virtual core ratio. However, memory-intensive applications and complex instruction sets may face performance bottlenecks due to the lower memory-to-CPU ratio and weaker memory benchmark scores. This instance is best suited for lightweight database operations, distributed caching, static web serving, and localized development tasks that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and fast local SSD storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of memory, a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, and a bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers average performance in stress-ng CPU benchmarks and Passmark database operations, though it exhibits weaker results in memory bandwidth and single-threaded decompression tasks. The dedicated core allocation and local NVMe storage make it a cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring fast local disk access and moderate compute power. It is well-suited for lightweight database operations, static web serving, and ARM-native application development.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD, suitable for lightweight database and web serving workloads.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for balanced, localized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 1 Gbps baseline network
Includes 237 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers average performance in stress-ng CPU and database benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight databases, static web serving, and ARM development
